options.why: Stopped at $x^2=400$ and did not take the square root.
Added the side lengths $12+16$ instead of squaring them first.
Subtracted the squares ($36-16$) instead of adding them.
Left the answer as $x^2=52$ without taking the square root.
Added the side lengths $4+6$ before rooting instead of the squares.
Did not take the square root of $106$.
Subtracted the squares ($81-25$) instead of adding them.
Truncated the third decimal instead of rounding it up.

options.text: He forgot to take the square root; $x=\sqrt{841}=29\text{ cm}$.

options.why: Subtraction finds a shorter side, but here the unknown is the hypotenuse.

options.text: He added the sides instead of squaring them.
options.why: He did square each side before adding, so this is not the mistake he made.

options.text: He used the wrong two sides.
options.why: Both given lengths are the shorter sides, which are the correct ones to add.
